steps.title: Use perpendicularity to find the tangent slope
steps.content:
The tangent line is perpendicular to the radius at the point of tangency. The radius goes from the center $(-1, 1)$ to the tangent point $(7, -6)$, so its slope is:
$$\text{radius slope} = \frac{-6-1}{7-(-1)} = \frac{-7}{8}$$
The tangent line has slope equal to the negative reciprocal:
$$\text{tangent slope} = \frac{8}{7}$$

steps.title: Write the equation of the tangent line
steps.content:
Using point-slope form with the point $(7, -6)$ and slope $\frac{8}{7}$:
$$y - (-6) = \frac{8}{7}(x - 7)$$
$$y + 6 = \frac{8}{7}x - 8$$
$$y = \frac{8}{7}x - 14$$
